sentences.text: Hey, what's up?

Not too bad.
Can't complain.

But um it's like it's like to get this kick off done.

Um Looks like we have everything built for her.
We just need users.
So that's the only thing I have um to, to bug them about.
Wonderful.
Yeah, this is a good account.
So hopefully get him uh get them rolling and get them spreading.
The good word is what we kind of need here.
Got it.
All right.
I'll let him in.
Cool.
Hey there, Alexis.
Can you hear us?
All right.
Yes, sorry,
I'm doing all right.
Just fine.
How's your Tuesday going?
It is going, it's not a Monday.
So you only go up from there.
I'm in the same boat.
I feel like it's too early to kind of tell if I'm having a good week or a rough week.
It's still, it's still early in the game here.
Um So my name is Phil Pinto.
I know you already know Austin.
Um But I'll be taken over as your point of contact here at apartment IQ.
Wonderful to get connected.
Where are you based out of
Alexis?
And I'm sorry, guys, my camera is not on today.
I am in the car.
So sorry guys.
All good.
All good.
I appreciate you and your flexibility and scheduling.
I do have a little bit of a presentation to work through, but I think
yeah, it shouldn't be too hard to follow.
I think on your phone if you're in the car.
Um, where are you driving to?
Are you in between on site visits or what?
Yeah.
So I was in Raleigh, Raleigh yesterday and then, uh,
high point today and I'm headed back to Columbia where I live today.
Wow, that is a lot of on the road.
I'm not jealous.
Uh, I myself a bit of a homebody so I do not envy all that travel for you.
It keeps me busy.
Yeah, definitely.
Definitely.
Well, thanks again, uh, for making time.
I wanted to make sure that we just got to meet each other.

I got to understand a little bit about what attracted you to apartment IQ and just make sure that we're doing everything that we can over the next couple of weeks, the next couple of months to make sure that this rolls out really smoothly to your team that everybody knows how to use apartment IQ.

And that I have a good understanding of what success looks like to you because what I'd like to be doing over time is connecting with you every quarter and just making sure that we're still hitting the goals that you expected to so that everybody's super happy at the end of the year.
Sounds fair enough.
Sounds great.
Cool.
So as far as uh the agenda meeting your apartment IQ team, that's me.
Of course, I am your primary point of contact here at apartment IQ.
Uh We do have our technical support team as well that is very available for any technical issues.
Any access changes, if your team needs to make changes to their comp sets, for instance, they're a great resource to help with that.
Uh And you and I will work together in strategic function to make sure that we're growing well together and that the product is working for everybody.
What I'd like to do is learn a little bit about why you've chosen Apartment IQ.
What was it that attracted you to Apartment IQ?
And what are you hoping Apartment IQ is gonna help you and the team do better this year?
Absolutely.
Um One of the things that kind of attracted me to you guys is the easiest and how easy you guys make it to get comp data and being accurate data.
Uh We get a lot of, oh, we don't do market surveys or we can't give you any information or they give us false information.
Uh So just making sure we're aligned with our costs that way we can make sure we're making, right.
Marketing recommendations, pricing recommendations, specials, all the good stuff to make us equal and uh give us an edge up from our costs.
Got it.
So, looking for that edge, it sounds like is your team really involved in the pricing recommendations or is that something you're doing?
Kind of what is the chain of command that we're talking?
So
it's kind of both.
Um I normally look at pricing recommendations each month and see where we can make tweaks, especially when it comes to like renewals.
Um And see.
Ok, well, where's our trend on this kind of floor plan and kind of basing it by floor plan to seeing what our trend is on that floor plan where we can make tweaks and bushes.
And then I always, but I always consult with my team first just to make sure they don't feel otherwise and just to make sure everyone's on the same page.
Yeah, that makes a lot of sense of team effort.
And I guess, are you uh are you reporting this upmarket kind of how are you measured month to month or, or quarter to quarter?
Absolutely.
So we have uh we submit market surveys to our clients each month with our financials goes out in mid month.
Uh But we also have weekly or provide weekly calls depending on the client uh where we're putting or showing updated market info and then what rent increases and changes we're making.
Got it.
Ok.
And everybody, I imagine these owners, they want to see the clients wanna see occupancy going up, they wanna see pricing going up.
Um, I, is that kind of how they're evaluating you and whether you're doing a good job with their properties?
Yeah.
Absolutely.
Excellent.
Excellent.
Um, let's talk about successful adoption.
We want everybody on the team to know what's going on in the market.
So, is it really important for you to see your team logging in weekly biweekly?
What would that look like if we were looking at a usage report, for instance,
um I would say biweekly.
Ok.
Makes a lot of sense.
There lines up with your midmonth uh reporting

and any big initiatives on the on the calendar this year for asset living for your business unit, any roadblocks or challenges that you would see potentially getting in the way here, something that we could be aware of and kind of playing around.
Not right this second, but things are changing every day.
So it does not mean that could not happen.
Yeah, absolutely.
And that's why I like to ask that.
I'll probably ask that just about every time we connect just to make sure if there is something crazy coming up.
Uh There's something your team is planning around.
We can kind of uh do our best to support in any way even if that's no support.
Um It just, it's good for us to be aligned in that sense.
Absolutely.
Um Thank you for the, the, the interview there.
I hope you don't mind me asking you a couple questions there, but it is helpful for me to just on.
Cool, cool, cool, cool.
Uh Moving on, right.
What is this onboarding time look like?
Uh When are you guys gonna be off into the races?
And where are we with building apartment IQ for you?
This is a very conservative timeline, not sure if you could make it out on your phone at this point.
Uh But we typically like to say that we can have you up and running in four weeks or less right now.
Uh We are having our kickoff call, but we're definitely further along than we are.
Most times I connect with my uh partners here.
So we already have your instance built.
The only thing we are missing is the users uh and who to add in.
So we have the shell ready.
Everything is ready to go.
We just need to get those users invited.
Um I'm I'll confirm with Austin, but I do think we are missing a list of users.
So if you could send that over to me, Alexis, that would be super helpful.
We can get those folks in tomorrow.
Um And then thank you.
Thank you.
The next step would be training of those users, right?
We have a weekly uh apartment IQ 101 training that we offer registration for I believe I sent you the link for that.
But I'll push that again your way just to make sure your teams have full access to that.
If those times aren't working, we can always find another one off training that'll be more personalized just for them as long as we can kind of align their calendars up together.
I think that's really helpful.
Otherwise that is, that is kind of all that's left is, is getting the team in getting them trained.
Then we're in this go live state, which is technically week four.
But I think it could be as soon as next week.
Uh And that would be uh we, we're handing off away from the support team per se, the implementation team and I become your personal point of contact here.

Uh Like I said, my role is to make sure that the strategy is aligned really well uh that you're getting everything you need out of apartment IQ and your teams are getting everything they need out of apartment IQ.
So our relationship looks like perhaps a monthly meeting over the first quarter.
That's what I'd like to recommend.
Just to make sure if there are any issues, we catch them early after that first quarter, we drop off to maybe a quarterly meet.

Just to say, hey, here are some updates, here's what's going on in our world, what's going on in the asset living side of things and then consistent and upcoming trainings for new features as they roll out.
So as there are major updates to apartment IQ, we'll be sure to reach out and say, hey, there are a couple new things.
Should we get the team together and walk them through?
And so we like to think of this as a big bear hug of support as we kind of go together in our partnership here.
Awesome.
One question for you.
Um who typically had access, Is it just the community managers that you see or is it the like entire on site?
So you see
that is a great question and a tough question for me to answer.
Um Just because it depends uh what's really lovely about apartment IQ you can have as many users as you need.
I probably had a baseline would recommend uh the leasing agent, the community manager get in there.
Um And then what's really nice is we can tailor their access.
So we just wanna make sure that everybody can read the information, we can set it such that they can't change anything.
Uh And those permissions perhaps with you or perhaps with their regional managers and, or middle managers there.
Um Just to make sure that we can't have anybody coming in and making changes to apartment IQ so we can really tailor it to your team.
OK.
All right.
Um Training, right?
What does that look like?
We could do the custom training session if we need to.
Otherwise we do have that apartment IQ one on one training weekly uh office hours.
You know, I like to do that at least quarterly just to make sure if anybody has any issues using apartment IQ.

Maybe they're used to seeing a certain piece of data expressed this way and they can't quite find it in apartment IQ we can get together in these office hours where they can drop in and say, hey, Phil, I'm looking for pre lease percentage.
I'm looking for uh the right way to calculate price per square foot on a one bed, 1.5 bath.
I can walk them through how to do that.
So that's always available
implementation.
We have your properties, we have your comps, it's just that list of users that we need and their role
and then finally that CS M handoff, right?
Our ongoing check ins our quarterly business reviews and then the constant support from our support team.
Any questions here about next steps and what our relationship is gonna look like.

I will send you um the list of employees this between this evening and this uh tomorrow morning.
As soon as I get back.

I think that's all we need before the before we're off to the races.
All right, Alexis,
perfect.

Terrific.
Thank you so much.
For your time.
Drive safe.
Of course, we'll talk soon.
Thank you guys.
Have a great day.
Bye now.
Thanks,
you too.
